name: Ansible Vault
description: Ansible Vault — built-in feature of Ansible for encrypting variables and files used in playbooks.

seo.title: Ansible Vault — Secrets Management
seo.meta_description: Ansible Vault: Ansible Vault — built-in feature of Ansible for encrypting variables and files used in playbooks.

when_to_use: Encrypting database passwords in Ansible playbooks
Protecting SSH keys and API tokens in infrastructure repos
Environment-specific secret injection into deploy workflows
Rotating secrets in place without plaintext exposure
Vault-encrypted variable files for multi-tenant deployments

pros: Free, bundled with Ansible, zero extra infra
Encrypts vars and files in-place with AES256
Simple CLI: encrypt, decrypt, edit, rekey
cons: No access control beyond the shared password
Rekeying is painful across many repos
No audit log or secret rotation features
Only useful inside the Ansible ecosystem
review.verdict: Fine for encrypting playbook vars, but not a real secrets manager for teams past a handful of admins.
review.best_for: Ops teams already on Ansible that need file-level encryption in Git.
review.not_for: Organizations needing audit trails, RBAC, or dynamic secrets.

lock_in.reason: OSS built into Ansible, fully portable
